name: Evaporated milk (food medium)
basis: per 100 g of canned, unsweetened evaporated WHOLE milk (~2.1x concentrated cow milk)

components.name: lactose
components.source: USDA FDC 11211050 (total sugars 10.0 g, all lactose in unsweetened evaporated milk)
components.evidence: DOMINANT fermentable sugar; milk sugar is essentially all lactose. mM assumes 100 g ~= 100 mL, MW 342.3.

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: Milk free-sugar literature (trace free glucose from lactose hydrolysis)
components.evidence: Trace free monosaccharide; not measured in USDA panel, included as trace.

components.name: D-galactose
components.evidence: Trace free monosaccharide from lactose; included as trace.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Milk citrate ~1.5-2.0 g/L (Garnsworthy/Variability of citric acid in cow milk, ResearchGate 304771887) scaled ~2.1x for evaporation
components.evidence: DOMINANT organic acid in milk; ~1.75 g/L in fluid milk concentrated ~2.1x. Range 0.25-0.37; some precipitates as Ca-citrate crystals on storage. MW 192.1.

components.name: orotic acid
components.source: Milk orotic acid ~50-100 mg/L (dairy composition literature) scaled ~2.1x
components.evidence: Characteristic pyrimidine acid of bovine milk. MW 156.1.

components.name: L-lactic acid
components.source: Fresh milk lactate 30-60 mg/L; J Dairy Sci S0022-0302(61)89935-9 method; scaled for concentration
components.evidence: Low in unfermented/heat-processed milk; variable. MW 90.1.

components.name: L-alanine
components.source: USDA milk amino-acid profile scaled to evaporated-milk protein 6.8 g
components.evidence: Computed from bovine milk AA profile x protein content.

components.name: L-arginine
components.source: USDA milk amino-acid profile scaled to protein 6.8 g

components.name: L-aspartate
components.source: USDA milk amino-acid profile scaled to protein 6.8 g

components.name: L-cystine
components.source: USDA milk amino-acid profile scaled to protein 6.8 g

components.name: L-glutamate
components.source: USDA milk amino-acid profile scaled to protein 6.8 g
components.evidence: Most abundant AA in milk (glutamate+glutamine, ~20% of protein).

components.name: glycine
components.source: USDA milk amino-acid profile scaled to protein 6.8 g

components.name: L-leucine
components.evidence: USDA reported value; major AA of casein.

components.name: L-phenylalanine
components.evidence: ADDED - was missing from current components.

components.name: L-proline
components.source: USDA milk amino-acid profile scaled to protein 6.8 g

components.name: L-serine
components.source: USDA milk amino-acid profile scaled to protein 6.8 g
components.evidence: Milk contains L-serine (current record lists 'D-Serine' in error).

components.name: L-tyrosine
components.source: USDA milk amino-acid profile scaled to protein 6.8 g

components.name: L-valine
components.evidence: ADDED - was missing from current components.

components.name: retinol (vitamin A)
components.evidence: Vitamin A; often fortified in canned evaporated milk.

components.name: beta-carotene
components.source: USDA milk carotenoid data (typical milk-fat beta-carotene)

components.name: vitamin D
components.evidence: Typically fortified in canned evaporated milk.

components.name: calcium
components.evidence: Concentrated ~2x vs fluid milk; casein-micelle + soluble citrate/phosphate. mM assumes 100 g ~= 100 mL.

components.name: chloride
components.source: Milk chloride ~1.1 g/L scaled ~2.1x (dairy chemistry, Tetra Pak handbook)
components.evidence: Major soluble anion; not in USDA panel, from dairy composition.

components.name: molybdate
components.evidence: Trace essential mineral; kept at trace level.

components.name: iodide
components.source: Milk iodine ~150 ug/L scaled ~2x (dairy trace-element literature)
components.evidence: ADDED - genuine milk trace mineral, absent from current record.

components.name: cobalt (Co2+)
components.source: Cobalt present as the metal centre of vitamin B12
components.evidence: Trace; essentially all as B12-bound cobalt.

components.name: cholesterol
components.evidence: Genuine animal sterol of milk fat - KEPT (dairy is an animal food).

components.name: butyric acid (C4:0)
components.source: Bovine milk-fat FA profile (~3-4% of fat) x 7.6 g fat
components.evidence: ADDED - characteristic short-chain milk fatty acid.

components.name: hexanoic acid (C6:0)
components.source: Milk-fat FA profile x 7.6 g fat

components.name: octanoic acid (C8:0)
components.source: Milk-fat FA profile x 7.6 g fat

components.name: decanoic acid (C10:0)
components.source: Milk-fat FA profile x 7.6 g fat

components.name: dodecanoic acid (C12:0)
components.source: Milk-fat FA profile x 7.6 g fat

components.name: myristic acid (C14:0)
components.source: Milk-fat FA profile (~10% of fat) x 7.6 g fat

components.name: palmitic acid (C16:0)
components.source: Milk-fat FA profile (~27% of fat) x 7.6 g fat

components.name: stearic acid (C18:0)
components.source: Milk-fat FA profile (~11% of fat) x 7.6 g fat
components.evidence: ADDED - major saturated milk fatty acid.

components.name: oleic acid (C18:1)
components.source: USDA FDC 11211050 monounsaturated 2.3 g; milk-fat oleic ~24%

components.name: linoleic acid (C18:2)
components.source: USDA FDC 11211050 polyunsaturated 0.25 g; milk-fat linoleic

remove.name: Sucrose C12H22O11
remove.reason: Evaporated milk is UNSWEETENED - it contains no sucrose; its sugar is lactose. FooDB/record mis-assignment. (Sucrose would only belong to SWEETENED condensed milk.) Replaced with lactose.

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: Milk serine is the L-enantiomer; D-Serine is a mapping error. Replaced with L-serine.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Nickel in milk is a trace contaminant (single-digit ug/L), not a growth nutrient; drop as non-nutritive trace element.

remove.name: CO2 CO2
remove.reason: Dissolved gas / matrix species, not a growth nutrient supplied by the food.

remove.name: O2 O2
remove.reason: Dissolved gas / environmental species, not a food-supplied nutrient.

remove.name: H+
remove.reason: Proton / pH bookkeeping species, not a nutrient.

remove.name: Ammonium
remove.reason: Not a defined nutrient of intact evaporated milk (trace NPN only); non-informative as a supplied component.

missing_added: lactose (the dominant fermentable sugar - was entirely absent; only erroneous sucrose was listed)
citric acid (dominant organic acid ~0.3 g/100g - organic acids were entirely missing)
major fatty acids: palmitic C16:0, oleic C18:1, myristic C14:0, stearic C18:0, butyric C4:0, linoleic C18:2 (only C6/C8/C10/C12 were present)
keep_uncovered: beta-carotene (genuine milk-fat carotenoid; likely no dedicated BiGG metabolite)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for a microbe growing on this medium are LACTOSE (~10 g/100g, ~292 mM) and CITRIC ACID (~0.3 g/100g, ~16 mM); casein/whey amino acids (glutamate/leucine/proline-rich) are the main N source. Proximates, minerals and vitamins are USDA FoodData Central 'Milk, evaporated, whole' (11211050); amino acids from USDA evaporated-milk records with the remainder scaled from the bovine-milk AA profile to 6.8 g protein; organic acids scaled from fluid-milk citrate/orotate/lactate by the ~2.1x evaporation concentration factor. Key corrections: added lactose + organic acids, removed erroneous sucrose, added the missing major milk fatty acids and Val/Phe.
